mission.title: Install and Configure Opengemini on Kubernetes
mission.description: openGemini is a cloud-native distributed time-series database designed for high concurrency, performance, and scalability. Installing it allows you to efficiently store and analyze massive telemetry data in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for openGemini to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy openGemini Components
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy openGemini in your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the openGemini pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have openGemini running in the 'opengemini' namespace with its pods in the 'Running' state. You should be able to access it via port 8080 on your local machine.

uninstall.title: Remove openGemini Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the openGemini deployment and service from the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade openGemini
upgrade.description: To upgrade openGemini, apply the new version manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n opengemini`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the endpoints with: `kubectl get endpoints -n opengemini`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: Verify that your cluster has enough resources (CPU and Memory) available. Use: `kubectl describe nodes` to check resource allocation.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
